protocol.name: wlr_foreign_toplevel_management_unstable_v1
protocol.copyright:
Copyright © 2018 Ilia Bozhinov
Permission to use, copy, modify, distribute, and sell this software and its documentation for any purpose is hereby granted without fee, provided that the above copyright notice appear in all copies and that both that copyright notice and this permission notice appear in supporting documentation, and that the name of the copyright holders not be used in advertising or publicity pertaining to distribution of the software without specific, written prior permission. interface.name: zwlr_foreign_toplevel_manager_v1
interface.description: The purpose of this protocol is to enable the creation of taskbars and docks by providing them with a list of opened applications and letting them request certain actions on them, like maximizing, etc.

event.name: toplevel
event.description: A new toplevel handle was created.

event.name: finished

description.summary: the compositor has finished with the manager

event.description: This event indicates that the compositor is done sending events to the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_manager_v1. The server will destroy the object immediately after sending this request, so it will become invalid and the client should free any resources associated with it.

request.name: stop
request.description:
Indicates the client no longer wishes to receive events for new toplevels. However the compositor may emit further toplevel_created events, until the finished event is emitted.
The client must not send any more requests after this one.

interface.name: zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1
interface.description: A zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 object represents an opened toplevel window. Each app may have multiple opened toplevels.

event.name: title
event.description: The title of the toplevel has changed.

event.name: app_id
event.description: The app-id of the toplevel has changed.

event.name: output_enter
event.description: This event is emitted when the toplevel becomes visible on the given output.

event.name: output_leave
event.description: This event is emitted when the toplevel stops being visible on the given output.

enum.name: state

description.summary: types of states on the toplevel

enum.description: The different states that a toplevel can have. These have the same semantics as the states with the same names defined in xdg-toplevel.

event.name: state
event.description: The states of the toplevel have changed. The given state is the new state. The received state can be set as an array of uint32.

event.name: done

description.summary: all information about the toplevel has been sent

event.description: This event is sent after all changes in the toplevel state have been sent. This allows changes to the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 properties to be seen as atomic, even if they happen via multiple events.

event.name: closed
event.description: The compositor sends this event to indicate that the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 will no longer receive events and should be destroyed.

request.name: set_maximized

description.summary: requests that the toplevel be maximized

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be maximized.

request.name: unset_maximized

description.summary: requests that the toplevel be unmaximized

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be unmaximized.

request.name: set_minimized

description.summary: requests that the toplevel be minimized

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be minimized.

request.name: unset_minimized

description.summary: requests that the toplevel be unminimized

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be unminimized.

request.name: activate
request.description: Request that this toplevel be activated on the given seat. The compositor may or may not honor this request.

request.name: close

description.summary: request that the toplevel be closed

request.description: Send a request to the toplevel to close itself.

request.name: set_rectangle

description.summary: the rectangle which represents the toplevel

request.description: The rectangle of the surface specified in this request corresponds to the place where the app using this protocol represents the given toplevel. It can be used by the compositor as a hint for animations.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 object. This request should be called either when the client does not want to use the toplevel anymore or after the closed event has been received to finalize the destruction of the object.

event.name: parent
event.description: This event is emitted whenever the parent of the toplevel changes.
